description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rough, angular form and a predominantly gray coloration. The surface texture is coarse, with visible grain and natural weathering, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for some time. The rock features a mix of flat and jagged faces, with one end tapering to a more pointed edge while the opposite side is broader and more block-like. Subtle variations in color, including lighter and darker gray patches, are present across its surface, likely due to mineral composition and weathering. The rock appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its size and shape make it suitable as a natural barrier or landscaping element in an urban environment.
description: A rough, angular gray rock with a coarse surface and natural weathering, featuring both pointed and blocky ends. Its solid, irregular form makes it suitable as an urban barrier or landscaping element.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's broadest, flattest face with a slightly pointed end on the right and a generally light gray coloration.
description_view_1: Here, the rock appears more block-like, with a rough, grainy surface and subtle color variations, highlighting its thickness.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the rock's angular, jagged edges and darker gray tones, showing more of its three-dimensional bulk.
description_view_3: The rock's pointed end is more prominent in this view, with a mix of rough and smoother surfaces visible along the sides.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
